text: Welcome to Fingerstyle Fundamentals Fall 2025! Please download the tablature packet in Materials before our first session. Bring your guitar tuned to standard (EADGBe).
Great first session everyone. This week practice the alternating-bass exercise from page 3 for 10 min daily. Focus on keeping the thumb steady — don't let the fingers rush it.
Week 3 reminder: we're adding the pinch pattern tonight. If you're still shaky on the alternating bass, that is OK — come anyway. We'll layer slowly.
Halfway point! Posted a short demo video of 'Freight Train' intro in Materials. Listen to Elizabeth Cotten's original recording first — her timing is everything.
No session this Monday (Oct 13 — holiday). We'll resume Oct 20. Use the week to consolidate the melody-over-bass exercise from last session.
Last session of the fall workshop next Monday Oct 27. Please bring the piece you want to share — even just 16 bars. No pressure on polish, just showing your progress.
Workshop complete — thank you all. It was a real pleasure. Travis Picking Intensive starts in January; enrollment link is in the pinned message. Stay in touch!
Welcome to Travis Picking Intensive! Before Session 1, please tune to standard and play through the warm-up exercise in Materials. We'll use it every week.
Session 2 recap: the key insight is that the thumb plays on beats 1 and 3, the fingers on the offbeats. Don't try to think about it — let it become automatic. Practice slowly.
For those working on 'Classical Gas': posted a phrase-by-phrase breakdown in Materials. Take it one four-bar chunk at a time.
Final session Feb 23 — share whatever you've been working on. Recordings are welcome if you can't make it in person. I'll post mine too.
Welcome to DADGAD Explorations! First thing: tune slowly — D A D G A D from low to high. Don't rush. Watch the demo video before Session 1 to hear what the tuning sounds like before you touch it.
Session 2: we covered the D modal drone voicing and the sus2-to-add9 movement. Tablature is now in Materials. Notice how much harmonic space you get for free just by strumming open strings.
Listening recommendation: check out John Renbourn's 'Lady Nothinge's Toye Puffe' and Pierre Bensusan's 'Pres de Paris' as reference points for DADGAD at its best.
Session 4 arrangement posted in Materials: 'The Water Is Wide' in DADGAD with a simple drone accompaniment. This is the piece we'll refine in Sessions 5 and 6.
Final session is May 18. Bring one piece — yours or mine — that you want to play through one time for the group. It's a sharing circle, not a recital.
Setlist for the Oct 24 gathering at Derek's is posted. Five songs, ~30 min. Chris will handle sound. Marcus: bring the larger body bass — the room is small but live.
Brookhaven Arts Festival DC — Nov 7. Load-in 10 AM, soundcheck 12:30, set at 2 PM. Travel logistics doc is in Materials. We have 45 min.
Fingerstyle Fundamentals Fall 2026 is now open for registration! Enrollment code: ff2026drg. Share with anyone who's been curious about guitar. Session dates will be confirmed by July 15.
Reminder: lesson scheduling for June is open. Use the Calendly link in Materials. 45-min slots available Tuesday and Thursday evenings.
New demo video posted: slow-motion thumb independence drill. Watch it at 0.5x speed and pay attention to what the wrist is doing.
